title: Editorial: Redefining ‘the Artist’ through New Narratives
content:
Dr Anna-Maria von Bonsdorff, Director, Ateneum Art Museum / Finnish National Gallery

25 March 2026

Finnish art is now more visible and present internationally than ever. Museums, such as New York’s Metropolitan Museum of Art, are acquiring Finnish art and Finnish art is present in many exhibitions in major museums around Europe and the US. This interest is growing. However, it also means that, as museum professionals and curators at the Finnish National Gallery, we are facing the fact that it is even more important that knowledge of Finnish history is expertly disseminated to our counterparts abroad, in addition to explaining the context the artists were and are working in. It also means a shift away from the emphasis on the solitary genius artist.

In this issue of FNG Research, there is a significant focus on ‘the artist’ and in several articles we highlight the many well-known names in Finnish art history, as well as new research and fresh approaches to these artists’ narratives. creator: Anna-Maria von Bonsdorff
sourceDescription: FNG Research is the online magazine of the Finnish National Gallery. This online publication aims to promote the research activities of the Gallery, research being a part of its core functions. The Finnish National Gallery consists of three museums – the Ateneum Art Museum, the Museum of Contemporary Art Kiasma, the Sinebrychoff Art Museum – and the Department for Collections Management. FNG Research is a magazine for art-museum and art-history professionals internationally. The site is updated bi-monthly with new developments and content.
